object_type: visible-sector and photon coupling interface for the DFM-MKC field-equation system
purpose: Supply the explicit matter and photon coupling rule required after DFM_MKC_FIELD_EQUATIONS_V1, while leaving perturbations, prediction vectors, and data comparison as downstream objects.

coupling_principle.name: metric-only minimal visible coupling
coupling_principle.statement: Visible matter and photons couple to the spacetime metric g_{mu nu}; they do not directly couple to phi or theta in V1.
coupling_principle.dark_sector_influence_route: The DFM-MKC sector affects visible matter and light only through its contribution to T_DFM_MKC_{mu nu} in the metric field equation.

ordinary_matter_coupling.visible_action: S_vis[psi_vis, g] = int_M d^4x sqrt(-g) L_vis(psi_vis, g)
ordinary_matter_coupling.stress_energy_definition: T_vis_{mu nu} = -(2/sqrt(-g)) delta(sqrt(-g) L_vis)/delta g^{mu nu}
ordinary_matter_coupling.test_particle_limit: u^mu nabla_mu u^nu = 0 for freely falling massive visible test bodies.
ordinary_matter_coupling.baryonic_limit: Baryons follow the metric geometry determined by G_{mu nu} + Lambda g_{mu nu} = 8 pi G (T_vis_{mu nu} + T_DFM_MKC_{mu nu}).
ordinary_matter_coupling.non_gravitational_visible_interactions: Contained inside L_vis; no new DFM-MKC-specific non-gravitational coupling is introduced in V1.

photon_coupling.maxwell_action_option: S_EM[A, g] = int_M d^4x sqrt(-g) [-(1/4) F_{mu nu} F^{mu nu}]
photon_coupling.field_strength: F_{mu nu} = nabla_mu A_nu - nabla_nu A_mu
photon_coupling.geometric_optics_limit: k^mu k_mu = 0 and k^mu nabla_mu k^nu = 0
photon_coupling.photon_path_rule: Photons follow null geodesics of g_{mu nu}.
photon_coupling.lensing_origin: Gravitational lensing is produced by the metric sourced by visible and DFM-MKC stress-energy.

geodesic_or_optical_rule.massless_probe_rule: k^mu k_mu = 0 and k^mu nabla_mu k^nu = 0
geodesic_or_optical_rule.optical_scalar_status: Optical-scalar and weak-lensing observables are deferred to the perturbation and prediction-vector objects.
geodesic_or_optical_rule.metric_source: g_{mu nu} is solved from the DFM-MKC field equations.

stress_energy_exchange_rule.visible_conservation_in_v1: nabla^mu T_vis_{mu nu} = 0 when visible equations hold and L_vis is diffeomorphism invariant.
stress_energy_exchange_rule.dark_sector_conservation_in_v1: nabla^mu T_DFM_MKC_{mu nu} = 0 when phi and theta equations hold.
stress_energy_exchange_rule.exchange_status: No direct non-gravitational energy-momentum exchange between visible matter and DFM-MKC fields is introduced.

equivalence_principle_status.weak_equivalence_principle: Weak equivalence principle preserved at the V1 matter-coupling level through universal metric coupling for visible test bodies.
equivalence_principle_status.einstein_equivalence_principle: Preserved for visible-sector local nongravitational physics under standard minimal coupling assumptions.

lensing_prediction_rule.statement: Lensing is computed from null geodesics of g_{mu nu}; DFM-MKC changes lensing only by changing the metric solution.
lensing_prediction_rule.strong_lensing_mapping_status: Deferred to later prediction-vector or case-study objects.
lensing_prediction_rule.bullet_cluster_relevance: A cluster-collision lensing comparison requires solving the coupled field system and is not supplied by this coupling rule alone.

radiation_limit.radiation_stress_energy: Standard traceless radiation stress-energy from minimally coupled photon fields.
radiation_limit.photon_speed: Null with respect to g_{mu nu}.

baryonic_limit.ordinary_baryon_dynamics: Baryonic matter follows the visible-sector equations from L_vis(psi_vis, g).
baryonic_limit.dark_effect_route: Baryons feel DFM-MKC only through the gravitational metric sourced by T_DFM_MKC_{mu nu}.
baryonic_limit.galaxy_rotation_curve_status: Not predicted here; requires a solved metric and matter distribution in a later prediction object.

downstream_pipeline_targets.role: Linearize the metric, phi, theta, visible matter, and photon sector around a cosmological background.
Generate reproducible ACT, Planck, and DESI-facing observable vectors.
Compare the frozen DFM-MKC prediction vector against Lambda-CDM on public data with a fixed likelihood and boundary ledger.

does_not_prove: DFM-MKC ACT Planck DESI prediction vector
next_admissible_step: Supply DFM_MKC_LINEAR_PERTURBATION_SYSTEM_V1 from the field equations and matter coupling rule.
